account-tracker-step-1-model-file.title: Step 1: Create the Account model file
account-tracker-step-1-model-file.prompt: Create `models/account.py` with an `Account` class. Keep `main.py` importing the model from the models folder.
account-tracker-step-1-model-file.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: Build a multi-file account tracker that demonstrates encapsulation, helper imports, and lists of objects.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

account-tracker-step-1-model-file.starterCode:
from models.account import Account

account = Account()
print("Account model ready")

account-tracker-step-2-constructor.title: Step 2: Add encapsulated starting state
account-tracker-step-2-constructor.prompt: Add `__init__(owner, balance)` and prevent negative starting balances by storing at least zero.
account-tracker-step-2-constructor.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

from models.account import Account

account-tracker-step-3-methods.title: Step 3: Add account behavior methods
account-tracker-step-3-methods.prompt: Add `deposit`, `withdraw`, and `summary` methods. Keep validation inside the class.
account-tracker-step-3-methods.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: Build a multi-file account tracker that demonstrates encapsulation, helper imports, and lists of objects.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

account-tracker-step-3-methods.starterCode:
from models.account import Account

account = Account("Ava", 125)
print(account.owner)
print(account.balance)

account-tracker-step-4-helper-formatting.title: Step 4: Create a formatting helper file
account-tracker-step-4-helper-formatting.prompt: Create `helpers/formatting.py`, import it in the model, and use it from `Account.summary()`.
account-tracker-step-4-helper-formatting.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: Build a multi-file account tracker that demonstrates encapsulation, helper imports, and lists of objects.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

account-tracker-step-4-helper-formatting.starterCode:
from models.account import Account

account = Account("Ava", 125)
account.deposit(25)
account.withdraw(40)
account.withdraw(999)
print(account.summary())

account-tracker-step-5-load-data.title: Step 5: Load accounts from a data file
account-tracker-step-5-load-data.prompt: Create `data/accounts.txt`, read rows from it, and turn each row into an `Account` object.
account-tracker-step-5-load-data.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: Build a multi-file account tracker that demonstrates encapsulation, helper imports, and lists of objects.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

account = Account("Ava", 125)
account.deposit(25)
account.withdraw(40)
print(account.summary())

account-tracker-step-6-final-summary.title: Step 6: Print the portfolio summary
account-tracker-step-6-final-summary.prompt: Finish the project by printing every account plus total account count and total balance.
account-tracker-step-6-final-summary.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: Build a multi-file account tracker that demonstrates encapsulation, helper imports, and lists of objects.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

account-tracker-step-6-final-summary.starterCode:
from models.account import Account

accounts = []
with open("data/accounts.txt", "r") as source:
    for line in source:
        owner, balance_text = line.strip().split("|")
        accounts.append(Account(owner, int(balance_text)))

for account in accounts:
    print(account.summary())

sc-module-8-account-tracker-project-proj.title: Check the design choice
sc-module-8-account-tracker-project-proj.prompt: Why does this module 8 account tracker project require each step to keep all files, not only the file changed in that step?
sc-module-8-account-tracker-project-proj.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Cumulative project steps must preserve the whole runnable app state.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.b: Because the next step starts from the full previous project state and must still run.
options.c: Because only README files are graded.
options.d: Because imports work even when files are missing.

project-story.title: Module 8 Account Tracker Project: project story
project-story.bodyMarkdown: This graded project is cumulative. Each step starts from the exact full solution of the previous step, so the project grows like a real multi-file app.
